data.title: The Memory Wave Reviews (URGENT REPORT) Neuroscience-Backed Audio Program Helping Adults Reclaim Memory in 12 Minutes a Day
data.description:
A 2026 Editorial Review of the 12-Minute Gamma Audio Program, User Results, Safety, Pricing, and Refund Protection.

WASHINGTON, D.C. / ACCESS Newswire / January 16, 2026 / If you've noticed moments of forgetfulness, slower recall, or mental fog creeping into daily life, you're not alone. In 2026, cognitive clarity has become one of the most sought-after wellness goals, especially among adults who want to stay sharp without pills, prescriptions, or complicated routines. That's exactly why the Official The Memory Wave has exploded in interest. It offers a radically simple promise: activate the brain's natural Gamma frequency using a 12-minute audio session designed to support memory, focus, and mental clarity.

What makes this different is not hype or novelty, but logic. Instead of adding chemicals to the body, The Memory Wave works with the brain's own rhythms using soundwave entrainment, an area of neuroscience that continues to gain attention for its safety and potential. With a 90-day money-back guarantee, the real question many people are asking isn't "does this work for everyone?" but rather "why wouldn't I test it for myself?"

>> Verified Source: Review full program details on the Official The Memory Wave Website

The Memory Wave Reviews Overview: Why This Gamma Audio Is Exploding in 2026

Search interest around The Memory Wave reviews has surged for one simple reason: people are actively looking for ways to protect and enhance their memory without committing to supplements, subscriptions, or medical interventions. In a crowded cognitive-health market, The Memory Wave stands out because it removes friction. No pills to remember. No formulas to trust blindly. No long routines. Just a short, daily audio session designed to support the brain's most important frequency for learning and recall.

What reviewers consistently highlight is accessibility. Users don't need prior experience with meditation, biohacking, or brain training. They simply listen. This ease of use lowers resistance and makes consistency realistic, which is often the missing piece in memory-support programs. Many users describe The Experience As Subtle But Undeniably Noticeable: clearer thinking, faster recall of names and words, and an increased sense of mental sharpness during conversations and work.

Importantly, positive reviews often come from people who went in with reasonable expectations. The Memory Wave is not marketed as a miracle cure, and users who treat it as a daily, effective cognitive support tool tend to report the best outcomes. This is consistent with how brain entrainment works. Gamma activation strengthens neural coordination over days if not weeks, not overnight.

>> Consumer Insight: Real-world usage expectations are outlined on the Official The Memory Wave Website

Criticism does exist, but when examined closely, most negative reviews fall into predictable categories. Some buyers misunderstood that the product is digital and expected physical delivery. Others tried it inconsistently or expected dramatic changes in days rather than weeks. These are not product failures; they're expectation mismatches. In fact, the presence of mixed reviews is often a credibility signal. It suggests real users with different baselines, not manufactured hype.

Another major driver behind the surge in reviews is the risk profile. With a 90-Day Refund Window, users know they're not locked in. This shifts buyer psychology. Instead of debating endlessly, many people simply try it. If it works, they keep it. If not, they get their money back. That's a rare dynamic in the cognitive wellness space and a big reason The Memory Wave continues to gain traction.

>> Editorial Verdict: A low-risk cognitive experiment backed by neuroscience logic, details available on the Official The Memory Wave Website

The Memory Wave Explained: How 12 Minutes of Gamma Soundwaves Target Memory & Focus

At its core, The Memory Wave is built around one scientific concept: Gamma brainwaves. Gamma is the fastest brainwave frequency and is strongly associated with memory formation, focus, learning speed, and overall mental clarity. When Gamma activity is low or disorganized, people often experience forgetfulness, brain fog, and difficulty concentrating. The Memory Wave is designed to gently guide the brain back into this optimal state using sound.

The program works through a process known as brainwave entrainment. When you listen to the audio through headphones, your brain naturally begins to synchronize with the embedded frequencies. This is not hypnosis and not stimulation in the medical sense. It's a passive alignment process that mirrors how music can influence mood or alertness, but with far more precision. The result is targeted Gamma activation without effort, strain, or learning curves.

>> Editorial Note: Readers who want to see exactly how The Memory Wave is positioned, priced, and protected by its refund policy should review the full details on the Official The Memory Wave Website.

What makes this approach compelling is its non-invasive nature. Unlike supplements that rely on digestion and absorption, or apps that require constant mental effort, The Memory Wave works regardless of age, fitness level, or prior experience. You don't have to "do" anything correctly. You simply listen for twelve minutes in a calm setting and let the audio do the work. This dramatically reduces the chances of user error, which is one of the biggest reasons many cognitive programs fail.

From a practical standpoint, the 12-minute format matters. Longer routines tend to break down over time. Short, repeatable habits stick. Users often integrate The Memory Wave into morning routines, pre-work sessions, or quiet evening moments. Over time, repeated Gamma activation supports smoother recall, clearer thinking, and improved mental stamina.

>> Updated Review Note: Usage Guidance And Listening Recommendations Are Detailed On The Official The Memory Wave Website.

It's also worth addressing safety. Because The Memory Wave uses audio only, there are no chemical interactions, no stimulant crashes, and no dependency concerns. For most adults, it represents one of the lowest-risk cognitive support tools available. This safety profile is a major reason many users feel comfortable experimenting with it, especially when paired with the refund guarantee.

In short, The Memory Wave doesn't ask you to believe in magic. It asks you to test a simple idea grounded in neuroscience: that when your brain is guided into the right frequency, clarity follows. With nothing more than headphones and twelve minutes a day, the barrier to trying it has never been lower.

>> Verified Source: Access The Full Explanation And Program Access Via The Official The Memory Wave Website.

The Memory Wave Science Breakdown: Gamma Brainwaves, MIT Research & Cognitive Clarity

The foundation of The Memory Wave rests on one of the most exciting developments in modern neuroscience: the role of Gamma brainwaves in memory, learning, and cognitive clarity. Gamma waves are the fastest brainwave frequency measured in humans, and they are consistently associated with high-level mental functions such as recall, focus, information integration, and mental sharpness. When people describe feeling "on," mentally clear, and quick to remember details, Gamma activity is usually elevated.

Research groups connected to institutions such as MIT have drawn attention to Gamma waves because of their link to what scientists call brain clearance. This is the brain's natural process of clearing metabolic waste that accumulates during thinking and aging. When Gamma activity weakens, this clearance becomes less efficient, often correlating with brain fog, slower recall, and reduced mental stamina. Strengthening Gamma rhythms, therefore, isn't about stimulation for stimulation's sake; it's about restoring a rhythm the brain already uses when it's functioning optimally.

>> Updated Review Note: The most current version of The Memory Wave, including any active bonuses or guarantee terms, is only accessible through the Official The Memory Wave Website.

What The Memory Wave does differently is accessibility. In lab settings, Gamma stimulation is achieved through controlled sound or light pulses. The Memory Wave translates this concept into a safe, home-based audio format. Instead of forcing the brain, it gently guides it using soundwave entrainment. The brain naturally synchronizes with repeated frequencies, a phenomenon documented for decades in neuroscience. This means users don't need to "try" or concentrate. The process happens beneath conscious effort.

Skeptics sometimes ask whether audio can really influence the brain. The answer is yes, not as a miracle, But As A Biological Response. Humans have always responded to rhythm: music can calm, energize, or focus the mind. The Memory Wave applies that same principle with precision. By embedding Gamma-range patterns into a 12-minute session, it creates conditions where the brain can re-enter a state linked to clarity and recall.

Importantly, this science does not promise identical results for everyone. That's normal. Brain health varies by age, sleep quality, stress levels, and lifestyle. But this variability doesn't weaken the logic; it strengthens it. It explains why a trial-based approach, backed by a 90-day refund guarantee, makes sense. You're not betting on hype. You're testing a neurological principle that already exists.

>> Verified Source: Full science overview available on the Official The Memory Wave Website

The Memory Wave Benefits: What This Audio Program Is Designed to Improve (And Why It's Not a Gimmick)

The real strength of The Memory Wave lies in how closely its benefits align with what Gamma brainwaves are actually known to support. This isn't a random list of promises. Each reported benefit connects logically to enhanced neural coordination and clarity, which is why many users describe the experience as subtle but meaningful rather than exaggerated.

One of the most commonly reported benefits is sharper recall. Users frequently mention that words come more easily, names feel more accessible, and details don't slip away as often. This makes sense. Gamma waves play a key role in how the brain binds information together. When Gamma activity improves, recall often feels smoother and more natural rather than forced.

>> Transparency Check: All usage instructions, delivery details, and refund eligibility are clearly outlined on the Official The Memory Wave Website, which helps explain why buyers feel comfortable trying it.

Another benefit is improved focus and mental stamina. Many people struggle not because they can't focus at all, but because their focus fades quickly. After consistent use, users often report being able to stay engaged in conversations, reading, or work tasks for longer periods without mental fatigue. This is especially valuable for adults who feel mentally "drained" by midday.

Importantly, The Memory Wave also supports emotional calm and confidence. This isn't marketed heavily, but it shows up in reviews. When memory and focus improve, stress often drops. People feel less embarrassed in social situations, less anxious about forgetting things, and more present overall. This emotional effect is a secondary benefit of cognitive clarity, not a separate claim.

Why this isn't a gimmick comes down to design and restraint. The program doesn't claim to cure diseases, replace medical care, or deliver instant transformation. It positions itself as a daily cognitive support tool. That honesty is why the benefits feel believable. And because it's audio-based, it avoids the most common pitfalls of supplements: absorption issues, side effects, and ongoing costs.

>> Consumer Insight: Benefit explanations and user expectations are outlined on the Official The Memory Wave Website

The absence of chemicals is also a benefit in itself. There's nothing to "cycle," nothing to stop suddenly, and nothing that interacts with medications. For many users, this safety profile is what makes trying The Memory Wave feel rational rather than risky.

The Memory Wave Results Timeline: What Users Report After Week 1, Day 30 & Day 90

Understanding when to expect changes is critical. Most dissatisfaction with cognitive tools comes from unrealistic timelines, not flawed products. The Memory Wave is designed to work with the brain's adaptive nature, which means results tend to follow a gradual, logical pattern.

Week 1 is usually about awareness, not transformation. Many users report subtle shifts: feeling slightly calmer, noticing moments of clearer thinking, or realizing that focus feels easier during certain tasks. These early changes aren't dramatic, but they're often enough to signal that something is happening beneath the surface.

>> Buyer Advisory: To avoid knockoffs or refund issues, verified access to The Memory Wave is available exclusively through the Official The Memory Wave Website.

By Day 30, the feedback becomes more consistent. This is when repeated Gamma activation begins to feel familiar to the brain. Users often describe improved recall speed, fewer "blank moments," and better mental endurance. At this stage, many people stop questioning whether the program works and start treating it as part of their routine.

The real consolidation happens closer to Day 90. This is where the design of the guarantee becomes important. Ninety days gives the brain enough exposure to reinforce new rhythms. Users who stick with daily sessions often report stable improvements rather than spikes. Memory feels more reliable. Focus doesn't require as much effort. Confidence in thinking becomes the new normal.

The Memory Wave Safety Review: Why This Digital Program Is Considered Low-Risk

One of the strongest reasons people feel comfortable trying The Memory Wave is its unusually low risk profile. Unlike supplements, stimulants, or invasive brain "hacks," this program does not introduce anything into the body. There are no chemicals to metabolize, no compounds to cycle, and no risk of interactions with medications or existing routines. From a safety standpoint, that alone places it in a completely different category than most memory products on the market.

The Memory Wave works through short, controlled audio sessions designed to gently guide brain activity rather than force it. This matters because overstimulation is where many cognitive tools fail. Long sessions, aggressive frequencies, or constant use can overwhelm users. Here, the experience is intentionally limited to about 12 minutes, which users consistently describe as calm, grounding, and easy to tolerate. Most report that it feels more like a reset than a stimulation.

>> Editorial Verdict: Given the low commitment and long refund window, many reviewers recommend evaluating The Memory Wave firsthand via the Official The Memory Wave Website instead of speculating on results.

Because it's a digital program, usage is fully optional and fully reversible. If someone feels tired, distracted, or simply doesn't want to listen on a given day, they skip a session. There's no dependency, no withdrawal effect, and no buildup in the system. That level of user control is one of the biggest safety advantages of this format.

The few minor issues occasionally mentioned by users are almost always related to user setup, not the program itself. Listening at very high volume or in a chaotic environment can cause mild discomfort, which is resolved immediately by lowering the volume or switching to a quieter space. There are no reports of lasting adverse effects when used as directed.

This is exactly why cautious users, including older adults and people who avoid supplements, often choose to try The Memory Wave first. It's a rare example of a brain-support tool where the downside risk is minimal, but the potential upside is meaningful.

>> Safety Overview: Usage guidelines and safety details are clearly outlined on the Official The Memory Wave Website

The Memory Wave Complaints Investigated: Refund Confusion, Expectations & User Errors

When people search for complaints about The Memory Wave, they often expect to uncover hidden flaws or serious red flags. What actually shows up instead is something very different. Nearly all complaints fall into three predictable categories: refund process confusion, unrealistic expectations, and simple user errors.

The most common issue involves refunds. The Memory Wave offers a long refund window, but some users fail to follow the correct steps or lose their purchase confirmation. This leads to frustration that has nothing to do with the program's effectiveness. When users keep their order details and follow the official refund process, refunds are issued without issue. This is a process problem, not a product failure.

>> Low-Risk Trial Note: You can review the full risk-free trial terms directly on the Official The Memory Wave Website.

Another frequent source of dissatisfaction is expectation mismatch. Some users go in expecting instant, dramatic changes after one or two sessions. When improvements show up gradually, as brain adaptation naturally does, they conclude too early that it "didn't work." Users who stick with the program for several weeks typically report clearer benefits, which aligns with how neural patterns strengthen through repetition.

User errors also account for a surprising number of complaints. Common examples include expecting a physical shipment instead of a digital download, missing the delivery email, or listening in noisy environments while multitasking. Once these issues are corrected, most users report a far better experience.

Buyer Awareness Note: Refund steps and delivery details are explained clearly on the Official The Memory Wave Website

What's important is what you don't see in complaint patterns: there are no credible reports of harm, no systemic billing scams, and no evidence of deceptive access. The complaints are logistical, not structural.

This is why many experienced buyers view The Memory Wave as a low-friction experiment rather than a high-stakes purchase. The refund window exists precisely to remove pressure and allow realistic evaluation.

Expectation Guide: Proper usage and realistic timelines are outlined on the Official The Memory Wave Website

The Memory Wave Scam or Legit? Transparency, Refund Policy & Trust Signals

The question "Is The Memory Wave a scam?" comes up for one simple reason: it sounds almost too easy. A short audio session promising better memory and focus naturally triggers skepticism. But when you break the product down structurally, it does not resemble scam operations at all.

First, transparency is clear from the start. The Memory Wave openly states what it is and what it is not. It does not claim to cure disease. It does not promise guaranteed results. It positions itself as a wellness-focused cognitive support tool and backs that positioning with clear usage instructions and disclaimers. Scam products rely on vagueness; this one does not.

Second, pricing and access are straightforward. There are no hidden subscriptions, no forced upsells, and no surprise renewals. It's a one-time digital purchase with lifetime access. That alone eliminates one of the most common consumer traps in this space.

>> Legitimacy Check: Program access, pricing, and guarantees are verified on the Official The Memory Wave Website.

Third, and most importantly, the refund policy is real. A long, no-questions-asked refund window significantly reduces buyer risk. Scam products avoid refunds or bury them behind impossible conditions. Here, the refund exists specifically to let users test the program without pressure.

Trust signals also include delivery control. The product is only available through one official source, which prevents counterfeits and ensures buyers are eligible for support and refunds. This centralized distribution model protects consumers rather than exploiting them.

When you put all of this together, the conclusion is straightforward: The Memory Wave is legitimate. That doesn't mean it will work identically for everyone, no cognitive tool does, but it does mean buyers are given a fair, transparent opportunity to decide for themselves.

The Memory Wave Pricing Analysis: Why a One-Time Fee Changes Buyer Psychology

Pricing is one of the most overlooked reasons The Memory Wave converts so consistently, and also one of the biggest reasons buyers actually use it. Unlike supplements, apps, or coaching programs that rely on monthly billing, this program is sold as a single, one-time payment. That structure fundamentally changes how buyers approach the product.

With subscriptions, users tend to procrastinate. They think, "I can always cancel later," which often leads to half-hearted usage and eventual abandonment. The Memory Wave removes that mental loophole. Once purchased, ownership is permanent. There's no ticking renewal clock, no recurring reminder of cost, and no guilt associated with "wasting another month."

Pricing Context: Current one-time pricing and included bonuses are shown on the Official The Memory Wave Website

Psychologically, this creates commitment. Buyers are far more likely to test the program properly when they know the decision is already made and the access is theirs. That commitment effect shows up clearly in user feedback. People who pay once and own it tend to build it into a routine, not treat it like a disposable app.

Another important factor is price anchoring. Most brain supplements cost $50-$80 per bottle and require ongoing reorders. Brain apps charge monthly or yearly fees that quietly add up. In contrast, The Memory Wave's one-time fee feels contained, finite, and controllable. Buyers know exactly what they're spending, and exactly what they're getting.

This pricing model also reduces pressure. When people don't feel trapped by a subscription, they're more open to experimentation. That's critical for a cognitive tool where gradual adaptation matters. Users feel free to test, pause, resume, and evaluate honestly, which increases trust and long-term satisfaction.

The Memory Wave Refund Policy Explained: Why the 90-Day Guarantee Favors the Buyer

Refund policies reveal more about a product's confidence than any sales page headline. In this case, The Memory Wave's 90-day guarantee is not a token gesture, it's strategically designed to favor the buyer.

Cognitive change doesn't happen overnight. Any program claiming instant, permanent results would be unrealistic. The 90-day window acknowledges that reality. It gives users enough time to listen consistently, experience adaptation, and evaluate results without being rushed into a verdict.

This longer refund period shifts the power dynamic. Instead of forcing buyers to decide quickly, it allows real-world testing under normal life conditions. Users can try morning sessions, evening sessions, busy weeks, calm weeks, and see how their mind responds across different contexts.

From a behavioral standpoint, this reduces buyer resistance at checkout. Knowing there's a long exit ramp lowers anxiety and increases willingness to try. Importantly, it also discourages impulsive refunds. When people don't feel pressured, they're more likely to give the program a fair trial rather than quitting prematurely.

Critically, the refund policy is straightforward. There are no hidden hoops, no usage limits, and no "must prove dissatisfaction" clauses. Buyers simply follow the official process within the window. That simplicity is a trust signal, and one that scam products never offer.

Risk-Free Trial Note: Refund eligibility and support access are confirmed on the Official The Memory Wave Website

The Memory Wave vs Supplements & Brain Apps: Why Audio Entrainment Is Different

To understand why The Memory Wave stands out, it helps to compare it directly with the two dominant alternatives in the brain-health market: supplements and brain apps.

Supplements rely on ingestion. That means digestion, absorption variability, potential interactions, and ongoing cost. Even high-quality formulas can take weeks to build up, and results vary widely depending on metabolism and consistency. For many buyers, this creates uncertainty and frustration.

Brain apps, on the other hand, demand effort. Games, puzzles, and exercises require sustained attention and motivation. Many users start strong but fall off after a few weeks because the process feels like work. The cognitive benefit is real, but adherence is poor.

The Memory Wave operates on a completely different mechanism. Audio entrainment is passive, not effort-based. There's nothing to solve, memorize, or complete. The user simply listens. That simplicity dramatically improves compliance, which is the real bottleneck in most cognitive programs.

Another key difference is mental load. Supplements add something to the body. Apps demand cognitive energy. The Memory Wave reduces mental friction. Users often describe sessions as calming rather than draining, which makes daily use sustainable.

Finally, there's permanence. Supplements and apps stop working the moment you stop paying or consuming. The Memory Wave remains available forever. That ownership model allows users to return to it during stressful periods, travel, or times of mental fatigue, without restarting costs or subscriptions.

This combination, passive use, no chemicals, no ongoing fees, is why many buyers view audio entrainment not as a replacement for everything else, but as a smarter foundation.

Comparison Insight: How The Memory Wave differs from pills and apps is explained on the Official The Memory Wave Website

The Memory Wave Who It's Best For (And Who Will Benefit the Most)

The Memory Wave is not designed for everyone, and that's actually one of its strengths. This program works best for people who value simplicity, consistency, and low-risk experimentation over pills, hacks, or complex routines.

The strongest results tend to come from adults who notice subtle but frustrating cognitive changes: slower recall, more "blank moments," mental fatigue, or reduced focus during conversations and reading. These users aren't looking for a miracle cure, they're looking to restore mental sharpness and confidence without disrupting their lifestyle.

It's especially well-suited for people over 40 and 50 who want to support memory and clarity without supplements. Many in this group actively avoid pills due to medication stacking or sensitivity concerns. Because The Memory Wave is audio-based, it sidesteps those issues entirely.

Professionals and creatives also fall squarely into the ideal user profile. People who rely on thinking speed, verbal recall, planning, or learning often report that the program fits seamlessly into their day. A short session in the morning or evening becomes a mental "tune-up" rather than another task on a long to-do list.

It's also a strong fit for cautious buyers. Anyone who hesitates to try new cognitive tools due to safety concerns typically feels more comfortable with a digital program that can be stopped instantly and tested under a long refund window.

Who benefits the most are those willing to use it consistently but patiently. The program rewards routine rather than intensity. Users who approach it as a daily habit, not a one-time trick, tend to report the most reliable improvements.

The Memory Wave Technology & Development Standards: Digital Delivery, Safety & Compliance

One reason The Memory Wave has gained traction is that it's built on controlled, digital delivery, not vague concepts or uncontrolled user input. The entire experience is standardized, which matters when you're dealing with brain-based tools.

The program is delivered as a digital audio file, meaning every user receives the same calibrated experience. From a safety standpoint, the technology is intentionally conservative. Sessions are short, repeatable, and designed to integrate into daily life without overstimulation. Users remain fully conscious and in control the entire time. There's no altered state, no dependency, and no lingering after-effects once the session ends.

Compliance also plays a role in trust. The Memory Wave does not present itself as a medical treatment or diagnostic tool. Its positioning is clearly within wellness and cognitive support, which aligns with how responsible digital programs are structured. That clarity protects both the user and the provider.

Digital delivery also solves a major consumer pain point: access. There's no shipping delay, no customs issues, and no risk of receiving the wrong product. Delivery is immediate, and setup requires nothing more than standard headphones and a quiet moment.

This combination, standardized audio, non-invasive use, and controlled session length, is why many users describe The Memory Wave as low-friction and low-stress, even compared to meditation or training apps.

The Memory Wave Final Verdict 2026: Why This Is a Smart, Low-Risk Cognitive Experiment

After walking through everything from science and user reviews to complaints, pricing, and expectations, the question you really want answered is: should someone actually try The Memory Wave?

The short answer is yes, and here's why that conclusion isn't hype, but a logical decision grounded in cognitive science, risk-benefit logic, and real-world user data.

First, let's talk about risk. The Memory Wave is low-risk by design. There are no pills, no chemicals, no interactions with other therapies, and no ongoing subscriptions. It's a short, guided audio experience that works with your brain's natural rhythms. For most adults looking to support clarity, memory, or focus, this makes it a safe first experiment, especially compared with long-term supplement regimens or demanding brain training routines that require extensive time or effort.

Second, the potential benefits align closely with what neuroscientists understand about Gamma brainwaves, the same frequency The Memory Wave targets. Although research is ongoing, multiple peer-reviewed studies suggest Gamma activity plays a key role in memory formation, attention, and neural synchronization. This scientific backing gives the program a conceptual foundation, not just a marketing story.

Third, the psychology of the offer tilts in your favor. A one-time fee changes how buyers commit and follow through, while a 90-day money-back guarantee removes nearly all financial risk. When you combine limited downside with plausible upside and a real refund structure, the rational choice, if you're curious about cognitive support, is to test it rather than endlessly debate it.

Finally, real users, especially those who gave the program a fair window, report improvements in recall, focus, and mental presence over time. These improvements aren't always dramatic or instant, but they're consistent with how brains adapt to repeated, gentle entrainment.

data.title: Emmecell Appoints Ramin Valian as Chief Executive Officer to Advance Magnetic Cell Delivery Platform
data.description:
Emmecell Appoints Ramin Valian as Chief Executive Officer to Advance Magnetic Cell Delivery Platform

Emmecell, a clinical-stage biotechnology company pioneering magnetic cell delivery technologies for ophthalmic disease, today announced the appointment of Ramin Valian as Chief Executive Officer. A veteran biopharmaceutical executive, Valian brings more than 20 years of global commercial and strategic leadership in eye care, with expertise spanning corneal disease, surgical and interventional glaucoma, retina, and consumer eye care. He has successfully led the launch of six ophthalmic products across therapeutics and medical technologies.

Valian joins Emmecell following a distinguished career at Allergan and AbbVie, where he played key roles in global strategy, clinical development, and commercialization of high-impact ophthalmic products. His decision to join Emmecell underscores his confidence in the company’s science, platform, and potential to deliver meaningful clinical impact for patients.

“I believe Emmecell’s magnetic cell delivery technology has the potential to fundamentally redefine ophthalmic treatment—from corneal disease to geographic atrophy,” said Ramin Valian, Chief Executive Officer of Emmecell. “The ability to precisely and non-invasively position therapeutic cells opens new possibilities for regenerative medicine. I’m honored to lead Emmecell as it advances toward pivotal clinical development and commercialization.”

Emmecell’s magnetic cell delivery (MCD) platform enables targeted placement of therapeutic cells through a minimally invasive procedure. The company’s lead program is being developed as a potential non-surgical alternative to corneal transplant, addressing limitations in donor tissue availability and surgical burden. Emmecell is also advancing a novel cell therapy for geographic atrophy, a significant unmet need in retinal disease.

“We are pleased to welcome Ramin to Emmecell,” said Jeffrey L. Goldberg, M.D., Ph.D., Co-Founder and Chairman of the Board. “His leadership experience and commitment to patient-centered innovation make him the right CEO for Emmecell as we advance programs in both corneal and retinal disease.”

As CEO, Valian will oversee corporate strategy, clinical and regulatory development, partnerships, and operational growth as the company prepares for pivotal cornea trials and commercialization.

About Emmecell

Emmecell is a clinical-stage biotechnology company developing magnetic cell delivery technologies to treat corneal disease, geographic atrophy, and other ophthalmic conditions. Its platform enables controlled, targeted positioning of therapeutic cells to support minimally invasive, regenerative alternatives to traditional surgical and pharmacologic therapies.

Noelia Kunzevitzky, PhD
Chief Operating Officer
Emmecell
info@emmecell.com
650-769-4232

data.title: Stuart Piltch Scholarship for Entrepreneurs Announces National Undergraduate Opportunity Focused on Customer-First Innovation and Business Leadership
data.description:
Stuart Piltch Scholarship for Entrepreneurs Announces National Undergraduate Opportunity Focused on Customer-First Innovation and Business Leadership

Stuart Piltch Scholarship for Entrepreneurs Announces National Undergraduate Opportunity Focused on Customer-First Innovation and Business Leadership

PR Newswire

NEW YORK, Jan. 16, 2026

NEW YORK, Jan. 16, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- The Stuart Piltch Scholarship for Entrepreneurs is now accepting applications from undergraduate students nationwide who demonstrate a strong commitment to entrepreneurship, innovation, and customer-focused business thinking. Established in honor of Stuart Piltch, the scholarship recognizes aspiring entrepreneurs who combine academic excellence with practical initiative and a clear vision for impact through business creation and problem-solving.

The Stuart Piltch Scholarship for Entrepreneurs is designed to support students across all fields of study who are actively pursuing entrepreneurial endeavors or who exhibit a strong entrepreneurial mindset.

Founded to reflect the values and professional legacy of Stuart Piltch, the scholarship emphasizes the transformative role of entrepreneurship in shaping industries and communities. Stuart Piltch, a respected entrepreneur and philanthropist, has built a distinguished career spanning the intelligence community and the healthcare sectors.

The Stuart Piltch Scholarship for Entrepreneurs seeks applicants who demonstrate academic achievement, a passion for entrepreneurship, and dedication to continuous personal and professional growth.

A core component of the application process is a written essay of fewer than 1,000 words. Applicants are asked to address the following prompt: "Discuss the importance of a customer-first focus in entrepreneurship. Describe how prioritizing the needs and wants of customers can contribute to the success of a venture and provide examples of entrepreneurs who have excelled in this area." This requirement reflects Stuart Piltch's belief that sustainable success in entrepreneurship is built on understanding and serving customers effectively.

In addition to financial assistance, the Stuart Piltch Scholarship for Entrepreneurs aims to foster a broader ecosystem of recognition and professional growth. The scholarship highlights the importance of collaboration, mentorship, and exposure to entrepreneurial resources that help students translate ideas into viable ventures. Through this initiative, Stuart Piltch continues to support the next generation of business leaders committed to responsible innovation.

The scholarship offers a one-time award of $1,000. The application deadline is September 15, 2026, and the scholarship recipient will be announced on October 15, 2026. Full details regarding eligibility, application requirements, and submission guidelines are available on the official website.

By advancing access to opportunity and recognizing entrepreneurial potential, the Stuart Piltch Scholarship for Entrepreneurs reinforces Stuart Piltch's ongoing commitment to education, innovation, and meaningful economic impact.

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/stuart-piltch-scholarship-for-entrepreneurs-announces-national-undergraduate-opportunity-focused-on-customer-first-innovation-and-business-leadership-302663685.html

SOURCE Stuart Piltch Scholarship

data.title: Colleges could go tuition-free if their endowment-fund managers just invested in this stock index
data.description:
By Gary Smith

Buy-and-hold S&P 500 investors outperform college endowment managers. Could this be why?

Last year, I proposed a straightforward solution to the crisis in higher education: Phase out teachers and students as colleges transition from educating students to helping administrators move up the administrator ladder.

Administrators decide whether to hire administrators or faculty - and it is not surprising that they prioritize themselves. From 1990 to 2022 (the most recent data I have), the number of tenured and tenure-track professors at my school, Pomona College, declined to 175 from 180 while the number of administrators (deans, associate deans, assistant deans and the like, not counting clerical staff, cleaners and so on) increased to 310 from 56. (The college's administration has stopped making this information publicly available. We can guess why.)

The tsunami of support I received for that article from "recovering academics" and other disillusioned professors confirmed that the trends I noted were not unique to Pomona College. Many cited examples of the bloat. One noted that his small college had a 12-person "Wellness Task Force" that organizes wellness events, such as a 45-minute walk around the campus. Another noted a 15-member administrative group that organizes events such as a "Winter Wellness Wonderland" where staff and faculty can write "Thank You Grams" to each other.

Neither event is inherently bad, but they surely don't require such large organizing committees. As a correspondent noted, "One thing I discovered when in the mid-90s I was for a couple of years in a somewhat senior administrative position (half-time, since I still taught each semester) - administrators needed to have their calendar full to prove how busy they were."

While no one - not even a school's trustees - can sway administrators from their belief that administrators are more important than faculty, a reader alerted me to the apparent existence of a so-far clandestine "cabal" that is evidently trying another strategy to slow the growth of administrators at schools with substantial endowments.

Trustees control how endowment funds are invested. The choice of mediocre investments can strangle the flow of endowment money to pay for yet more administrators.

Endowment income is the largest single course of revenue for many schools. For fiscal year 2025, 37% of Harvard University's total operating revenue, for example, came from distributions from its endowment. For Yale and Princeton universities and Pomona College, the respective numbers were 33%, 55% and 49%.

Schools typically target a distribution of 4% to 5.5% of the market value of the endowment (or the average value over a few years), expecting that an annual investment return of 7% to 8% will maintain the endowment's real, inflation-adjusted value. If the real value of the endowment grows over time because of more profitable investments, then additional revenue becomes available to support administrative bloat.

If, on the other hand, the endowment returns are stifled, the hiring of new administrators will be restrained, if not stopped entirely. This is evidently what the investment cabal has done. The table shows the realized investment returns in fiscal 2025 and for the 10-year period 2016-2025 for the eight Ivy League colleges, plus Stanford University, the University of Chicago and MIT. No college did as well as the S&P 500 SPX in 2025 - or for the 10-year period 2016-2025.

This dismal performance might be intentional. Consider how much larger each of these college's endowments would have been in 2025 if they had simply invested in the S&P 500 and withdrawn a constant 5% of of the endowment each year. (These figures do not include the resources used to manage these disappointing investments.) 

   Average  12.1  9.2   42.4  9.7 
   S&P 500  15.2  13.7  N/A   14.4

The market value of Harvard's endowment on June 30, 2025 was a staggering $56.9 billion. If instead it had invested in the S&P 500 for the past 10 years, the endowment would have been 66% higher, at $94.5 billion. Using a 5% withdrawal rate, the extra $37.6 billion of endowment would generate $1.9 billion in annual revenue, enough to support an additional 12,518 administrators being paid an average annual total compensation of $150,000. Those 12,518 additional administrators were effectively blocked by the mediocre endowment returns.

(Harvard's net tuition and fees, after deducting financial aid and scholarships, is around $1.3 to $1.4 billion - which means that they could have used the additional endowment income to eliminate tuition and fees entirely - though this would not have been a high priority for the university's administrators.)

Pomona College's endowment is a mere $3 billion, but if it had invested in the S&P 500 for the past 10 years, its endowment would have been about 68% higher. (This is an approximation because the college has not yet publicly announced the results for the 2025 fiscal year. An additional $2.04 billion in endowment would generate an additional $102 million in annual revenue, enough to support 680 more administrators or, as with Harvard, effectively eliminate all tuition and fees.

The other schools in the table fall in the range between 680 and 12,518 additional administrators. Without their perverse investment strategies, we would be well on our way to that utopia of all-administrator schools.

Perhaps there is another explanation for the mismanagement of endowment portfolios. One argument is the mantra that investing in the S&P 500 creates too much year-to-year volatility in the market value of the endowment and this will spill over into the annual endowment withdrawals that support the schools' budgets.

For example, a trustee on one investment committee emailed me that, "Most people would find it strange to compare endowment overall with the S&P as a benchmark. Most folks use a 70/30 portfolio (70% stocks/30% bonds) as the right benchmark. With the way the endowment is used to support the budget and all the administration, it probably wouldn't be prudent to have 100% equity allocation."

Thus, most schools invest in a diverse set of mediocre investments in order to offset the volatility of the stock market. Evidently mediocrity loves company. As John Maynard Keynes observed, "Worldly wisdom teaches that it is better for reputation to fail conventionally than to succeed unconventionally."

There are three major flaws in this argument. One is that these other investments do a lousy job of stabilizing endowment returns. Post-1950, the worst single fiscal year for the S&P 500 was 2009, when the annual return was -25.5%. Yale's 2009 return that year was -24.6%; Harvard's was -27.3%; Pomona's -23.0%.

The second flaw is the misplaced concern with short-term performance. Portfolios that are 60/40, 70/30, and the like are explicitly intended to achieve "the growth potential of stocks combined with the stability of bonds." However, the inclusion of bonds or other diversifying assets focuses on the wrong objective. Growth matters much more than stability.

After the Yale endowment's disappointing 1.8% return in 2023, compared to the S&P 500's 19.8%, Matthew Mendelsohn, Yale's Chief Investment Officer, wrote that "As a 322-year-old institution, Yale benefits from the rare ability to invest with a truly long time horizon. Given this, we measure our success over decades, not days, months, or even years."

That argument is exactly correct - and exactly why endowment managers should little care about dampening year-to-year volatility. They should be looking at long-term performance, which is where stocks excel. In August 2016, I wrote that stocks were an attractive long-term investment: "I don't know what will happen to stock prices over the next few months or years, but, ten years from now, in 2026, I expect value investors who were fully invested in August 2016 to be pleased with the results."

The third flaw is the belief that the only way to stabilize endowment withdrawals is to stabilize the market value of the endowment. Schools can instead use stabilizing withdrawal rules; for example, a fixed percentage of the average value of the endowment over the past 10- or 20 years. Another simple rule is to ignore the market value of the endowment completely and withdraw an amount equal to the average income (or some multiple of the average income) generated by the endowment over the past 10 or 20 years.

The people managing these endowments are highly intelligent and well-informed. I choose to believe that their poor performance is entirely intentional.

Gary Smith is emeritus professor of economics at Pomona College. He is the author of more than 100 academic papers and 20 books, including "Standard Deviations: The Truth About Flawed Statistics, AI and Big Data" (Duckworth, 2024) and (co-authored with Margaret Smith) "The Power of Modern Value Investing: Beyond Indexing, Algos and Alpha" (PalgraveMacmillan, 2024).

-Gary Smith

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal. 




  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  01-16-26 1441ET


Copyright (c) 2026 Dow Jones & Company, Inc.

data.title: PotlatchDeltic Announces Tax Treatment for 2025 Dividend Distributions
data.description:
PotlatchDeltic Announces Tax Treatment for 2025 Dividend Distributions

PotlatchDeltic Corporation (Nasdaq: PCH) announced today the tax treatment for its dividend distributions made in 2025 on the company’s Common Stock (CUSIP# 737630103).

The dividend distributions, totaling $1.80 per share, are classified for income tax purposes as 100% Capital Gain Distributions (long-term 20% rate).

The table below summarizes the income tax treatment of the company’s 2025 dividends:

Shareholders are encouraged to consult with their tax advisors as to their specific treatment of PotlatchDeltic distributions.

(1) Form 1099-DIV box 2a. Pursuant to Treas. Reg. § 1.1061-6(c), the one-year and three-year amounts disclosures are $0.00 with respect to direct and indirect holders of “applicable partnership interests,” as all capital gain dividends relate to IRC Section 1231 gains.

About PotlatchDeltic

PotlatchDeltic Corporation (Nasdaq: PCH) is a leading Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) that owns 2.1 million acres of timberlands in Alabama, Arkansas, Georgia, Idaho, Louisiana, Mississippi, and South Carolina. Through its taxable REIT subsidiary, the company also operates six sawmills, an industrial-grade plywood mill, a residential and commercial real estate development business and a rural timberland land sales program. PotlatchDeltic, a leader in sustainable forest management, is committed to corporate responsibility. More information can be found at www.potlatchdeltic.com.

data.title: AbbVie, Genmab Lymphoma Treatment Shows Improved Progression-Free Survival
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan 




  Topline results from a trial evaluating AbbVie and Genmab's epcoritamab to treat a type of lymphoma demonstrated an improvement in progression-free survival, the companies said. 



  AbbVie and Genmab said Friday the trial results also showed improvement in patients' complete response rate, duration of response and time to next treatment. 



  The study didn't demonstrate a statistically significant improvement in overall survival, however, the companies said. 



  The study enrolled 483 patients with relapsed or refractory diffuse large B-cell lymphoma with at least one prior line of therapy who were ineligible for high-dose chemotherapy and autologous stem cell transplant. 



  Further analysis of the results is continuing, the companies said. 



  Genmab and AbbVie plan to engage with global regulatory authorities to discuss next steps. The data will also be submitted for presentation at a future medical meeting, they said. 



  "The results from this global trial contribute to the growing body of evidence supporting epcoritamab," Genmab's Chief Executive Jan van de Winkel said. 



  Epcoritamab has previously received regulatory approval for certain lymphoma indications in more than 65 countries. 





  Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com 





  (END) Dow Jones Newswires



  January 16, 2026 14:25 ET (19:25 GMT)

data.title: Historic Battleground Country Club Joins Concert Golf Partners Portfolio
data.description:
Historic Battleground Country Club Joins Concert Golf Partners Portfolio

Historic Battleground Country Club Joins Concert Golf Partners Portfolio

PR Newswire

MANALAPAN, N.J., Jan. 16, 2026

Storied land near the site where George Washington led the Continental Army
in the Revolutionary War's Battle of Monmouth in 1778,
now home to the 40th club in the Concert Golf portfolio

MANALAPAN, N.J., Jan. 16, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Battleground Country Club, a 220-acre upscale private club located between New York City and Trenton, New Jersey, has joined the Concert Golf Partners family of premium private clubs as its 40th property, further expanding Concert's presence across the Philadelphia–New Jersey–New York corridor.

Situated near the site of the Revolutionary War's seminal Battle of Monmouth in 1778, Battleground Country Club offers its members a rare blend of historical significance and modern private club amenities. George Washington is said to have surveyed his troops from a hill near the club, rallying the Continental Army during the historic engagement.

Battleground's owners, the Maschler Family and Michael McCarty, selected Concert Golf Partners to carry forward the club's legacy. Their decision underscores Concert's reputation as the preferred successor for thriving, high-end private clubs seeking long-term stewardship, continuity, and a seamless transition for members. With substantial financial resources and a long-term ownership philosophy, Concert is uniquely positioned to invest in premier clubs while preserving their distinct culture and traditions.

"Concert Golf is deeply committed to upholding the high standards and traditions that define our clubs, and Battleground's remarkable history and engaged membership make it a natural fit within our portfolio," said Concert Golf Partners Senior Vice President Jordan Peace. "We seek out exceptional private clubs where thoughtful investment and a member-first approach can further elevate the overall club experience."

"We are tremendously proud of what we have built at Battleground Country Club since we took ownership in 2009," said Battleground Co-Owner Erik Maschler. "We are confident that Concert Golf Partners is the right steward to continue investing in the Club as they offer our members a standard of service that enriches and enhances Battleground's storied tradition. We will forever be grateful for the many memories and relationships we have made at the Club."

Based near Orlando, Concert Golf Partners is a boutique owner and operator dedicated solely to private clubs. By investing its own capital and partnering closely with on-site management teams, Concert strengthens club operations, enhances amenities, and delivers an elevated member experience—while preserving the identity and traditions that make each club unique.

Designed by Hal Purdy in 1961, and later re-routed and renovated in the early 2000s by the Northeast Collaborative led by Robert McNeill, Battleground's par-71 golf course stretches nearly 7,000 yards and carries a rating of 73.7, with a slope of 133. The club also features two swimming pools, four tennis courts, a cigar lounge, and multiple dining venues. Its Colonial-style clubhouse accommodates weddings and special events for up to 500 guests.

Legendary CBS Sports Commentator, Jim Nantz worked at Battleground Country Club in the 1970s for then head pro Tony Bruno - an experience that helped shape Jim's lifelong passion for golf.

Concert Golf Partners is highly selective in expanding its portfolio, focusing on premier private clubs near major metropolitan markets. The company is well known for the positive impact it brings to club communities and surrounding property values through long-term investment and thoughtful stewardship.

Concert Golf has 40 properties nationwide from coast-to-coast, reaching from The Club at New Seabury on Cape Cod to TPC Monterey at Pasadera in Monterey, California. Battleground is located approximately 45 minutes from TPC Jasna Polana (Princeton, N.J.), which Concert Golf Partners added to its portfolio in 2024. Among the other prominent Concert Golf properties in the Northeast: Penn Oaks Golf Club and White Manor Country Club (near Philadelphia); The Muttontown Club (near New York City); and Blue Hill Country Club (near Boston).

View original content to download multimedia:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/historic-battleground-country-club-joins-concert-golf-partners-portfolio-302663666.html

SOURCE Concert Golf Partners
